subject: A spiny orange-amber sea creature resting on a wet sandy tidal flat surrounded by dark rocks
description: A ground-level wide shot of a shallow tidal flat at low tide. The central subject is a striking elongated sea creature with radiating amber-orange spines — it lies on wet sand between scattered dark rounded rocks, its body oriented diagonally across the frame. The creature resembles a large spiny nudibranch, sea slug, or starfish-like organism. Thin strands of green algae or seaweed trail across the sand. The background is dominated by a large dark green mass — either a wave or rock formation — and the scene has a muted, overcast quality. The palette is cool blue-greens and grays against the warm amber-orange of the creature, which pops as the sole warm element in the composition. No figures present.
foreground: wet sand, dark rounded rocks, thin algae strands
background: dark green wave or rock formation, overcast sky
emotion: still, quiet; the creature at rest on exposed shore — contemplative or alien
